# Transcrição: 05-How to Check Client Site

- **Fonte:** 05-How to Check Client Site
- **Duração:** 03:25
- **Idioma:** en
- **Modelo:** small

---

## [00:00 - 03:02]

Alright, one of the important things to do, guys, is we want to make sure that while we are going to be running campaigns and sending people to Messenger using offline event tracking, all of that good stuff, we still want to be able to track as much as possible as we possibly can within the online assets that our client has, okay? So you can even track, you can even set this up for a phone call. So if your client has a phone number on their website, it gets a bit trickier, but there's stuff that you can do. But one of the basic things that you should be looking at is actually installing a standard event after an appointment or an inquiry form on your client's website. So your existing client's website, okay? So for example, this is a hair salon. If you're working with an accountant, it might be like Inquire or Get in Touch or whatever. We're going to click on appointments. You'll see I've got the Pixel Tool Helper here to help me see what kind of stuff they have on this website. So we're going to go book a salon appointment. And you're basically going through the entire funnel. Okay. So you can see here, once I've gone through the funnel, they're using an app called Square App as their booking system. There needs to be a redirect to another landing page because at the moment this is not, this is not triggering any tracking whatsoever. So it's going to make it a little bit tricky for us. If, for example, people are looking at the ads, they decide to check out the website or they decide to check out the Facebook page, then they go to the website and they book through the website. It means that none of that is going to get attributed back to our ads. It's only going to happen once we track it with offline event tracking. However, even then the accuracy is only going to be about 70%. So what I would advise this client at this stage is try to work through a situation with them where we can see if this particular app can redirect once someone confirms, like ScheduleOnce does this, right? Can redirect to a separate page on their website where we have the standard event lead that says, hey, thank you so much, et cetera, et cetera, and ideally embed this stuff in there as well so people can add this to their calendars.

## [03:03 - 03:25]

Or if this app actually allows tracking right from the get go using Facebook. So this is an important part of prepping the client account. Try and at least make this work because if you don't, a lot of what you think is not, you're, it's not going to report properly, right? You're going to be missing a lot of leads of people that actually went through the website.
